name: Kobe
summary: Kobe is one of Japan's safest major cities, with extremely low crime rates and excellent infrastructure. However, visitors should be aware of natural disaster risks, particularly earthquakes, as the city sits on active fault lines and was devastated by the Great Hanshin Earthquake in 1995.
quickVerdict: Kobe is exceptionally safe for tourists, ranking among the world's safest cities with minimal violent crime and reliable emergency services. The main risks come from natural hazards like earthquakes and typhoons, but the city has state-of-the-art warning systems and disaster preparedness infrastructure. Minor nightlife district scams and petty theft are rare but possible. Overall, travelers can feel very secure exploring this beautiful port city.

neighborhoods.name: Sannomiya
neighborhoods.description: Central business district with excellent safety, well-lit streets, and heavy foot traffic. Minor pickpocketing risk in crowded areas but generally very secure day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Kitano Ijinkan-gai
neighborhoods.description: Historic foreign quarter with upscale atmosphere, tourist police presence, and minimal crime. Well-maintained streets and good lighting make it very safe for sightseeing.

neighborhoods.name: Harborland
neighborhoods.description: Modern waterfront district with shopping and dining. High security presence and good lighting, though crowded areas require standard precautions against pickpocketing.

neighborhoods.name: Arima Onsen
neighborhoods.description: Traditional hot spring resort area in the mountains. Very safe with minimal crime, though access roads can be challenging during severe weather conditions.

neighborhoods.name: Nagata-ku
neighborhoods.description: Residential area with slightly higher petty crime rates than other districts. Generally safe during daylight hours but exercise extra caution at night in less populated areas.

neighborhoods.name: Hyogo-ku Port Areas
neighborhoods.description: Industrial port district that's generally safe but can be poorly lit at night. Avoid isolated areas after dark and stick to main thoroughfares when possible.

scams.name: Nightlife Overcharging
scams.description: Street touts in entertainment districts may invite visitors to bars with extremely inflated prices or spiked drinks.
scams.howToAvoid: Only visit pre-researched establishments, never follow street promoters, pay with cash only in nightlife areas, and never leave drinks unattended.

scams.name: Fake Photo Request
scams.description: Someone asks you to take their photo, then deliberately drops the camera and demands payment for damages.
scams.howToAvoid: Politely decline photo requests from strangers, or if you must help, be extremely careful with their device and have witnesses present.

scams.name: ATM Card Skimming
scams.description: Helpful strangers at ATMs offer assistance while secretly scanning cards and watching PIN entry.
scams.howToAvoid: Always cover your PIN with your free hand, decline help at ATMs, use bank-operated machines inside branches when possible.

scams.name: Overpriced Tourist Shopping
scams.description: Some shops near tourist areas may charge inflated prices for souvenirs or use high-pressure sales tactics.
scams.howToAvoid: Research typical prices beforehand, compare costs at multiple shops, and don't feel pressured to buy immediately.

soloFemale.overview: Kobe is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, with Japan ranking among the world's safest countries for women. Street harassment is virtually non-existent, and women regularly travel alone at night without incident. The biggest concerns are natural disasters and minor nightlife scams that affect all tourists equally.
tips: Carry emergency contact information in Japanese
Use well-lit main streets even though crime is minimal
Download translation apps for easier communication
Be cautious in nightlife districts and decline street promoter offers
Register with your embassy for disaster alerts

nightSafety.overview: Kobe is very safe at night with minimal violent crime, though some entertainment districts require standard precautions. The city is well-lit and patrolled, with many residents and workers out late. The main nighttime risks are overcharging scams in bars and rare instances of drink spiking in tourist-heavy nightlife areas.
tips: Stick to well-established bars and restaurants
Avoid following street promoters to unknown venues
Travel in groups in entertainment districts when possible
Keep emergency numbers saved in your phone
Use licensed taxis or public transport late at night

transport.metro: Kobe's public transport system is extremely safe and reliable, with JR lines, Hankyu, and Hanshin railways serving the city. Trains run on time with excellent safety records, though they can be crowded during rush hours. Crime on public transport is virtually non-existent.
transport.rideshare: Uber operates as a taxi-hailing service in Kobe, connecting users with licensed drivers. Japan introduced limited ride-sharing in 2024, with plans to expand to Kobe. All drivers are properly licensed and vehicles inspected, making these services very safe.
transport.taxis: Taxis in Kobe are extremely safe, clean, and honest with functioning meters. Drivers are professional and many vehicles have GPS tracking. Companies like MK Taxi and Kamome Taxi offer English-speaking services. No tipping is expected and overcharging is virtually unknown.
transport.tips: Use the GO app for taxi booking, public transport is safer and cheaper than taxis, avoid unlicensed vehicles, and keep train station maps handy as English signage varies.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes, traditional restaurants, and temples
Bow slightly when greeting rather than handshaking, though both are acceptable
Don't tip - it's considered rude and staff may chase you thinking you forgot money
Use both hands when receiving business cards and examine them respectfully
Cover your mouth when coughing or sneezing, and wear a mask if feeling unwell
Don't eat while walking - consume food and drinks while seated or stationary
Be quiet on public transport and put phones on silent mode

health.overview: Kobe has world-class healthcare with modern hospitals and clinics. The city recovered completely from the 1995 earthquake and now has excellent medical infrastructure. Many facilities have English-speaking staff, and emergency services are efficient and well-equipped.
health.water: Tap water is completely safe to drink throughout Kobe and meets strict quality standards. No water purification needed.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for Japan. Standard travel vaccines (hepatitis A/B, typhoid) may be recommended but are not mandatory.
health.altitude: Kobe is at sea level with a temperate climate. Summer temperatures can reach 32°C with high humidity, while winters average around 5°C. No altitude-related health concerns.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Consulate General Osaka-Kobe: +81-6-6315-5900, 2-11-5 Nishitenma, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-8543. Emergency after-hours: +81-6-6315-5900

faq.a: Yes, Kobe is extremely safe for tourists with very low crime rates. The main risks are natural disasters like earthquakes, but the city has excellent warning systems and disaster preparedness infrastructure.
Kobe is very safe at night with minimal violent crime. Exercise standard precautions in entertainment districts where overcharging scams may occur, but overall nighttime safety is excellent.

faq.q: Is Kobe safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, Kobe is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers. Street harassment is virtually non-existent and women regularly travel alone without incident, even at night.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Kobe?
faq.a: No areas are particularly dangerous, but exercise extra caution in Nagata-ku at night and avoid isolated port areas after dark. Entertainment districts require standard precautions against overcharging scams.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Kobe?
faq.a: Yes, Kobe's public transport is extremely safe with virtually no crime reported. Trains and buses are clean, reliable, and well-maintained with excellent safety records.
